reasoning: As of October 26, 2025, despite severe tensions and the US launching at least seven deadly strikes against presumed civilian "drug vessels" off the coast of Venezuela since early September, there has been no direct kinetic engagement with the Venezuelan National Bolivarian Armed Forces (FANB) or militia. Similarly, no strikes have hit Venezuelan land territory. Both sides appear to be carefully avoiding a direct state-on-state military clash. With only 5 days remaining until the October 31 deadline (a third of the time that was left on October 16 when the market was at 0.48), a simple time-decay model implies the probability should drop to roughly 19%. Given the established pattern of U.S. strikes focusing strictly on non-military targets and Venezuela limiting its response to diplomatic condemnation, a direct military escalation in the very short remaining window seems increasingly unlikely, warranting a lower probability of 15%.
